3GPP_Reference.Title: NG-RAN F1 Application Protocol (F1AP), NG-RAN Overall Description; Stage 2

Feature_Description: The UE Context Setup procedure is used to establish the UE Context, including SRB, DRB, BH RLC channel, Uu Relay RLC channel, PC5 Relay RLC channel, and SL DRB configuration. This specific procedure is used for the case when the UE moves from one gNB-DU to another gNB-DU within the same gNB-CU during NR operation for L1/L2 Triggered Mobility (LTM).

The gNB-CU initiates the procedure by sending a UE CONTEXT SETUP REQUEST message to the candidate gNB-DU. If the gNB-DU successfully establishes the UE context, it replies with a UE CONTEXT SETUP RESPONSE message. If no UE-associated logical F1-connection exists, it shall be established as part of the procedure.
Feature_Objectives: To establish the UE Context at the candidate gNB-DU for Inter-gNB-DU LTM handover.
To configure LTM-specific resources and parameters at the candidate gNB-DU, such as CSI resource configuration, LTM configuration ID mapping, and lower layer reference configurations.
To enable early synchronization (early TA acquisition) at the candidate gNB-DU for the LTM procedure.
To receive the generated LTM configuration and early UL sync information from the candidate gNB-DU.

Protocol_Layers: F1-U (User Plane - implied by bearer setup)

Feature_Implementation_Steps: 1. The gNB-CU determines to initiate LTM configuration based on L3 Measurement Reports from the UE.

2. The gNB-CU sends a UE CONTEXT SETUP REQUEST message to the candidate gNB-DU(s) for each candidate cell. This message contains the candidate cell ID and the CSI resource configuration for subsequent LTM. The gNB-CU may also provide the LTM configuration ID mapping list and request PRACH resources or lower layer configuration for reference generation.

3. If the candidate gNB-DU accepts the LTM configuration request, it responds with a UE CONTEXT SETUP RESPONSE message, including the generated lower layer RRC configurations for the accepted target candidate cell.
Procedure_Implementation_Steps: 1. The UE sends a MeasurementReport message (L3 measurement result) to the source gNB-DU containing measurements of neighbouring cells. The source gNB-DU sends an UL RRC MESSAGE TRANSFER message conveying the received MeasurementReport message to the gNB-CU.
2. The gNB-CU determines to initiate LTM configuration.

3. The gNB-CU sends a UE CONTEXT SETUP REQUEST message to the candidate gNB-DU(s) for each candidate cell, containing one candidate cell ID and the CSI resource configuration for subsequent LTM. The gNB-CU may provide the LTM configuration ID mapping list to the candidate gNB-DU(s). The gNB-CU may request PRACH resources from the candidate gNB-DU(s). The gNB-CU may request the candidate gNB-DU(s) to provide the lower layer configuration for the purpose of generating the reference configuration or provide the lower layer part of the reference configuration to the candidate gNB-DU(s).

4. If the candidate gNB-DU accepts the request of LTM configuration, it responds with a UE CONTEXT SETUP RESPONSE message including the generated lower layer RRC configurations for the accepted target candidate cell.

Code_Instructions.description: Data structure defining the format of the F1AP UE Context Setup Request message. This is crucial for both the gNB-CU (to construct the message) and the gNB-DU (to parse the message).
Data structure defining the format of the F1AP UE Context Setup Response message. This is essential for the gNB-DU (to construct the message) and the gNB-CU (to parse the message).

A helper function likely responsible for encoding or preparing the F1AP UE Context Setup Request message from RRC/MAC layer information for transmission over the F1 interface. This function would be called by the gNB-CU to construct the message payload.

A utility function within the gNB RRC layer (CU) responsible for generating the content or specific information elements required for the F1AP UE Context Setup Request message. This function helps populate the data structure before encoding.
A helper function likely responsible for decoding or processing the received F1AP UE Context Setup Response message. This function would be called by the gNB-CU to parse the response from the gNB-DU.

Information_Elements.IE_Definition:
UEContextSetupRequestIEs F1AP-PROTOCOL-IES ::= {
    { ID id-LTMInformation-Setup                                 CRITICALITY reject    TYPE LTMInformationSetupIE                                 PRESENCE OPTIONAL},
    { ID id-LTMConfigurationIDMappingList                       CRITICALITY reject    TYPE LTMConfigurationIDMappingListIE                      PRESENCE OPTIONAL},
    { ID id-EarlySyncInformationRequest                         CRITICALITY reject    TYPE EarlySyncInformationRequestIE                        PRESENCE OPTIONAL},
    { ID id-SpCellID                                            CRITICALITY reject    TYPE SpCellIDIE                                           PRESENCE OPTIONAL},
    ...
}

LTMInformationSetupIE ::= SEQUENCE {
    lTMIndicator                                                LTMIndicatorIE,
    spCellID                                                    SpCellIDIE,
    referenceConfiguration                                      ReferenceConfigurationIE                                OPTIONAL,
    csiResourceConfiguration                                    CSIResourceConfigurationIE                              OPTIONAL,
    ...
}

Piggy_Backed_IES_Info: The UE CONTEXT SETUP REQUEST message for Inter-gNB-DU LTM handover can carry several LTM-specific configurations. These include the LTM Information Setup IE, which contains the LTM Indicator, SpCell ID, and optionally, reference configuration details (request for lower layer configuration or the configuration itself) and CSI resource configuration. Additionally, the LTM Configuration ID Mapping List IE can be provided to the candidate gNB-DU. The Early Sync Information Request IE can also be included to request early TA acquisition information.
